---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

<!--
================================================================================
parac1_file_sorting.Rmd
================================================================================

# Helper utility: PA-Rac1-specific file sorting variant.
# Same body shape as helpers/file_sorting.Rmd but specialized for the
# PA-Rac1 imaging batches (different filename pattern).

## What this file does

Same per-cell subfolder sorting as ``file_sorting.Rmd`` but reads the
PA-Rac1 imaging filenames (which use the ``paRac1_mVenus`` /
``200730_TC_proto*`` naming convention). The file-pattern regex is
adjusted accordingly.

## Manuscript panel

**None — utility helper.** Like its sibling ``file_sorting.Rmd``, this
file is the pre-analysis sort step that runs once per imaging dataset
to organize TIFF files into per-cell subfolders.

## Why it lives in helpers/

The PA-Rac1 datasets use a different filename convention from the
Lifeact / actin radial flow datasets, so a slightly customized
sorting helper was needed. Kept here as a sibling utility.

## Beginner notes

Knit-time destructive of the working directory (physically moves
TIFF files). Run only on a fresh PA-Rac1 imaging directory that needs
to be sorted.

## Original G:\ source

G:/Figure 3_Arp3/Fig. 3j-n_PARac1/Andor/PARac1/201215_paRac1_mVenus/200730_TC_proto20201215_174813_20201216_182439/sorting.Rmd

================================================================================
-->

```{r}
library(filesstrings)

Path_1 <- dirname(rstudioapi::getSourceEditorContext()$path)

file_list <- list.files(path =Path_1, full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)
file_list <- file_list [ grepl("tif", file_list) ]
file_list_df <- file_list %>% as.data.frame()
for (n in 1:25){
  
  newfolder <- paste("Cell_", formatC(n, width=2, flag="0"), sep = "")
  dir.create(newfolder)
  
}

subfolder_list <- list.dirs(path =Path_1, full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)
subfolder_list  <- subfolder_list [ grepl("Cell", subfolder_list) ]

file_list_df$path<- subfolder_list

for (n in 1:length(file_list)){
 
  file.move(file_list[n], file_list_df$path[n])
  
}

```
